title: asparagus officinalis proles prostratus
intro: Asparagus officinalis proles prostratus, also known as creeping asparagus or prostrate asparagus, is a type of perennial plant in the asparagus family that has a creeping or prostrate growth habit. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of asparagus officinalis proles prostratus include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting kidney function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is typically harvested in the summer months when the plant is in full growth.
